message:
perf(vdbe,core): hot-opcode fast lane, MakeRecord pre-sizing, cursor caching, connection tuning

Major VDBE interpreter and connection-layer performance improvements:

## Hot-opcode fast lane

The five most frequently executed opcodes (Column, ResultRow,
Eq/Ne/Lt/Le/Gt/Ge comparisons, Compare, and MakeRecord) are now
dispatched through a dedicated `try_execute_hot_opcode` method that
runs before the main match statement. This method is marked
`#[inline(always)]` and each handler is a separate inlined method,
giving the compiler maximum opportunity to optimize the hot path
without the branch prediction pollution of the full 100+ opcode
match.

The hot path includes:
- `execute_column_hot`: direct column-to-register extraction
- `execute_result_row_hot`: result row materialization with metrics
- `execute_comparison_jump_hot`: comparison + conditional jump with
  null handling, STOREP2 mode, and collation support
- `execute_compare_hot`: multi-column Compare with per-field collation
- `execute_make_record_hot`: record serialization (moved from inline)

## Fast same-storage-class comparison

A new `fast_compare_same_storage_class` function provides O(1) type-
checked comparison for the common case where both operands have the
same storage class (Integer-Integer, Float-Float, Text-Text, Blob-Blob).
For Text-Text comparisons with builtin collations (BINARY, NOCASE,
RTRIM), it bypasses the collation registry lock entirely via
`builtin_collation_compare_text`.

## MakeRecord buffer pre-sizing

`estimate_make_record_buffer_capacity` scans the program's MakeRecord
opcodes at execution start and pre-allocates the scratch buffer to
the largest estimated record size. The estimate uses column affinity
hints: fixed-width columns (INTEGER/REAL) contribute 9 bytes each,
variable-width columns (TEXT/BLOB) contribute page_size/16 bytes
(clamped to 64..512). This eliminates per-row Vec reallocation for
the common case. The buffer uses `truncate(0)` instead of `clear()`
to preserve capacity across statements.

## Cursor rowid and payload caching

StorageCursor gains two new cached fields:
- `cached_rowid`: avoids repeated B-tree rowid lookups when the
  cursor position hasn't changed (Column opcode reads it multiple
  times per row for IPK alias detection)
- `payload_includes_rowid_alias`: caches the result of the
  `payload_includes_rowid_alias_lazy` check per row position

Both caches are invalidated when the cursor moves or its row layout
is refreshed.

## SetSnapshot cold path extraction

The SetSnapshot opcode handler (time-travel cursor upgrade) is moved
to a `#[cold]` method `execute_set_snapshot_cold`, removing 140 lines
from the hot interpreter loop. This improves instruction cache
utilization for the common non-time-travel path.

## Connection cache size tuning

Compiled bytecode cache, prepared statement cache, and planner
directive cache are all increased from 128 to 256 entries. The
shared connection counter is plumbed through `SharedMvccState` to
enable the pager's single-connection fast path.

## B-tree cursor formatting

Minor rustfmt fix in btree cursor.rs: long match arm split across
lines for readability.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
